bench: monorepo scale test (Spec K, docs/specs/bench-scale-runnable.md — runnable via bench/experiments/scale.mjs)

graph.note: nodes/edges/domains/overlaps byte-match the pre-refresh graph at the same pin — the engine's output on this corpus is unchanged since the 0.8.x measurement; earlier 'files: 709' counted scanned files, filesWithSymbols counts files that own nodes

oneFileEdit.note: the re-map-after-an-agent-edit cost Spec O exists to cut; optimize's 60.5s here vs 28.8s cold is allocation/GC churn on the same work, not different work

queries.note: per-call subprocess incl. 13.5MB graph parse; in-process MCP serves from cache

reportAtScale: bench/results/report-scale.json — 16k-symbol report GREEN in headless Chromium: graph interactive 217ms, layout settled 4.8s, search 22ms, heap 31MB, no errors (Spec L)

theWall.verdict: optimize.mjs — 36% of cold, 83% of the one-file-edit re-map
theWall.mechanism: 112 duplicate-logic candidates each run applyEdit (clones the 16k-node graph) + fileCycles (full file-graph SCC on the clone) + impactOf — O(candidates x graph) with heavy allocation; ~260ms per candidate at this scale
theWall.downstreamSplit: cluster 0.7s + overlap 1.2s + report 0.8-2.2s — the caps landed in Spec B hold; overlap is no longer the wall
theWall.coldExtract: 47.4s — AST parse + Type-3 fingerprints + reliance v2 over 544 files; rides the scan cache thereafter (8.2s on the edit pass, 6.9s no-change)

previous.note: superseded figures, measured by hand on a DIFFERENT HOST at engine 0.8.x — cross-host totals are not comparable; kept for the receipt trail
discoveredAndFixed: catastrophic regex backtracking in shingles.tokenize on unterminated-quote content — an input-dependent HANG (overlap sat at 100% CPU >15 min on one 2-node group); replaced with a linear-time matcher + regression test

quadratic twin seeding through hub labels and unbounded same-name pairwise confirmation — bounded by declared caps (hub >50 callers, 200k pair budget, 12-node group samples, 400-line body shingles), all reported in overlap.md

shardsDecision.rationale: 16k symbols load monolithically as a 13.5MB graph with ~200ms subprocess queries and a 4s memoized re-map; 10x this scale still fits memory comfortably. The answer-preserving sharding contract was never wired to any CLI/MCP surface and the measured need does not exist; git history preserves the implementation if a 100k+-symbol case ever materializes.

previous.findingsSpotCheck: top findings are real compiler duplication (substituteExpression x9 drifted, transformConstructorBodyWorker x3 at 80% body) — 46 ready merges, ~1757 LOC, gate-green
